reasoning: To resolve NO, the word "tariff" or "tariffs" would need to appear on the front print page of AT LEAST ONE of the two newspapers (The New York Times or the Wall Street Journal) EVERY SINGLE DAY from the open date until June 1, 2025. Given the typical variety of front-page news (spanning international affairs, domestic politics, social issues, accidents, and features) and the fact that we have a window of over a month (incorporating weekends and typically slow news days), the mathematical probability of 'tariff' showing up sequentially without a single day of omission across both papers is vanishingly small. Even during peak trade war periods, it is extremely rare for a specific policy noun to consistently monopolize front pages without break. Consequently, it is highly likely that there will be at least one day where both newspapers omit the word from their print front pages, fulfilling the YES resolution criteria.
batch_queries: Will the word "tariff(s)" disappear from the front print pages of The New York Times and Wall Street Journal by June 1, 2025?
